codeReview.summary: Re-run fix review result: 3/4 prior blocking findings are resolved (bulk validation messages, raw-material manufacturing/expiry capture, and frontend handoff flow corrections). The remaining blocker from decompose-finished-goods-service is still unresolved because business logic is centralized in FinishedGoodsWorkflowEngineService (2119 lines) while the focused catalog/stock/reservation/dispatch services remain pass-through wrappers.

issues.description: Decomposition blocker remains unresolved: core finished-goods business logic is still concentrated in a single 2119-line engine service (wc -l), including catalog, reservation, and dispatch operations (e.g., listFinishedGoods line 101, createFinishedGood line 221, reserveForOrder line 298, confirmDispatch line 814).

Focused services are still thin delegators and do not contain migrated domain logic: FinishedGoodsCatalogService/FinishedGoodsStockService/FinishedGoodsReservationService/FinishedGoodsDispatchService each hold a workflowService field and forward methods directly to FinishedGoodsWorkflowEngineService.

Frontend data-contract section is partially stale after this fix: RawMaterialBatchRequest and RawMaterialIntakeRequest bullets omit newly-added manufacturingDate and expiryDate fields, even though these fields now exist in DTOs and are used in intake/receipt flows.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker decomposition procedure can still be satisfied by moving logic into a new engine/core class while keeping focused services as wrappers, which conflicts with mission decomposition intent and repeatedly permits superficial decomposition outcomes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill text in .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md says to keep the god service as a facade initially; this fix produced FinishedGoodsWorkflowService as a 147-line facade, but moved logic into FinishedGoodsWorkflowEngineService (2119 lines) and left focused services as delegators.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Documentation verification guidance is still incomplete for DTO field drift: changed request DTOs may not be fully mirrored in frontend-handoff data contracts.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: DTOs now include manufacturingDate/expiryDate (RawMaterialBatchRequest.java line 15-16, RawMaterialIntakeRequest.java line 16-17), but frontend-handoff data-contract bullets at lines 1323 and 1325 still list old field sets without those fields.

summary: Fail. This fix closes three prior blockers, but it does not resolve the finished-goods decomposition failure because logic remains centralized in a new 2119-line workflow engine with focused services still acting as pass-through wrappers.
